title: Provide an easy way to install certificate authority on Podman Machine
body:
### Feature request description

If we need to access an internal registry with Podman, using Podman Machine, we have to [manually install the certificate authority](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-install-certificate-authority.md). For such a common use case for corporate users, it should be an easier way to do it.

### Suggest potential solution

Add a subcommand to `podman machine`, like `podman machine install-ca /path/to/cert.pem`.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Added in 3f88370:

When creating a new machine:

```podman machine init --import-native-ca```

To enable it on an existing machine:

```podman machine set --import-native-ca```

I run podman on a windows 11 via powershell but the parameter seems not to be active:
I'll stick to --tls-verify=false for the moment.

From Powershell:
```
podman --version && podman machine init --import-native-ca
podman version 5.8.2
Error: unknown flag: --import-native-ca
See 'podman machine init --help'
```

It's also not listed when i type "podman machine init --help":
```
podman machine init --help
Initialize a virtual machine

Description:
  Initialize a virtual machine

Usage:
  podman machine init [options] [NAME]

Examples:
  podman machine init podman-machine-default

Options:
      --cpus uint              Number of CPUs (default 8)
      --disk-size uint         Disk size in GiB (default 100)
      --ignition-path string   Path to ignition file
      --image string           Bootable image for machine
  -m, --memory uint            Memory in MiB (default 2048)
      --now                    Start machine now
      --playbook string        Run an Ansible playbook after first boot
      --rootful                Whether this machine should prefer rootful container execution
  -s, --swap uint              Swap in MiB
      --timezone string        Set timezone (default "local")
      --tls-verify             Require HTTPS and verify certificates when contacting registries (default true)
      --usb stringArray        USB Host passthrough: bus=$1,devnum=$2 or vendor=$1,product=$2
      --user-mode-networking   Whether this machine should use user-mode networking, routing traffic through a host user-space process
      --username string        Username used in image (default "core")
  -v, --volume stringArray     Volumes to mount, source:target (default [...])
```

@AquilaRapax, can you run ```podman machine set --import-native-ca``` after creating the VM? I am also running podman version 5.8.2 on Windows 11.

Might be worth opening a new issue if ```machine set``` works but ```machine init``` doesn't.

@dbristow-otc And does importing the ca work on your machine? I tried your suggestion but to no avail. It's the same error 
```
podman machine set --import-native-ca
Error: unknown flag: --import-native-ca
```
For now, I solved the issue by importing a certificate bundle in the machine directly, so it is not an urgent issue, but it would be much easier if this parameter would work.

I'm getting the same error with Podman 5.8.2, both on `podman machine init` and `podman machine set`:

```
❯ podman --version
podman version 5.8.2
❯ podman machine init --import-native-ca
Error: unknown flag: --import-native-ca
See 'podman machine init --help'
❯ podman machine set --import-native-ca
Error: unknown flag: --import-native-ca
See 'podman machine set --help'
```

The official [_latest_ documentation says there's this flag](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-machine-set.1.html#import-native-ca), but it doesn't show up in the 5.8.2 version: https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.8.2/markdown/podman-machine-set.1.html
